analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet contrasts China with some U.S. politicians over the 'source of the virus', labeling U.S. accusations as lies and prejudice, and amplifies a Xinhua line about a 'political virus' smearing China. This portrays the U.S. as hostile and deceitful while positioning China as aligned with truth and science. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent) — Yes. The tweet accuses U.S. politicians of lies and prejudice and of smearing China, depicting the West/US as hostile and unethical toward China. CH2 (China is a benevolent power) — No. It does not highlight cooperative or multilateral benevolence; it focuses on rebutting U.S. attacks. CH3 (China has an epic history) — No. No historical resilience theme. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values) — Partially implied via 'truth and science' alignment, but it does not explicitly advocate China's political system or domestic values; focus is external criticism, so No. CH5 (The government of the Chinese Communist Party succeeds) — No. No claims about governance success. CH6 (China is interesting culturally/naturally) — No. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH1.
